issues.description: TSX is missing the data-pagination-mode and data-post-type attributes on the section element. PHP outputs these as data attributes (data-pagination-mode, data-post-type) on the section wrapper, but the TSX does not include them in useBlockProps. While these are only used by frontend JS (not in the editor), the TSX/PHP structural parity is broken.
issues.fix: Add data-pagination-mode={attributes.paginationMode} and data-post-type={attributes.blockVariation} to the useBlockProps className object, or pass them as extra attributes to useBlockProps.
issues.alsoUpdate: N/A - PHP is correct, TSX needs updating

issues.description: HTML entity encoding breaks Tailwind CSS class. The class [&:has(button:only-child)]:hidden on .archive-active-filters (Archive.php line 284) is rendered as [&:has(button:only-child)]:hidden in the browser. WordPress encodes the & character to &, which prevents the Tailwind CSS selector from matching. As a result, the active filters container (which should be hidden when only the Clear All button is present and no filter pills exist) will always be visible, showing a stray 'Clear All' button even when no filters are active.
issues.fix: Replace [&:has(button:only-child)]:hidden with a custom CSS class defined in Archive.css that uses the :has() pseudo-class directly. For example, add .archive-active-filters:has(button:only-child) { display: none; } to Archive.css, and remove the Tailwind class from the PHP template.

issues.description: No data present for team_member, resource, policy, and testimonial variations. All four show 'No results found' because no posts of these custom post types exist in the database. While the block code is structurally correct and handles the empty state gracefully, the test page does not demonstrate card rendering for these variations. The card templates cannot be verified as rendering properly without actual post data.
issues.fix: Create sample posts for each CPT (team_member, resource, policy, testimonial) with populated meta fields and taxonomy terms so the test page can demonstrate all card templates rendering properly.

issues.description: Blog post card has no featured image displayed. The 'Hello world!' post has no featured image set, so the card renders without an image container. While the template handles this case (conditional check with has_post_thumbnail()), the clip-path image styling and alternating layout cannot be verified without images present. The blog card's accent color also shows 'Uncategorized' with no accent_color term meta, so the accent color system cannot be validated.
issues.fix: Set a featured image on the test blog post, assign it to a category that has accent_color term meta set, and create additional blog posts to test the alternating card layout and accent color per-card feature.

issues.description: Query var 'per_page' conflicts with WordPress default. The query var 'per_page' is registered in archive-query-vars.php and used in Archive.php line 118 and the JS. However, 'per_page' is a common REST API parameter and could cause conflicts. More importantly, since query vars are global, having multiple archive blocks on the same page (as the test page does) means they all share the same per_page query var. Changing per_page on one block instance via AJAX affects all blocks on page reload.
issues.fix: Consider using a block-instance-scoped approach for per_page, or document that per_page is a global state. For multiple blocks on a single page, a unique identifier per block instance could be used to namespace query vars.

issues.description: Blog card date format shows literal 'Date (Month DD, YYYY)' with the word 'Date' prepended. The spec describes the format as 'DATE (MONTH DD, YYYY)' which is the literal text shown in the Figma mockup. The current implementation at card-post.php line 45-50 uses printf with 'Date (%s)' format. This is likely intentional from the Figma design, but the text 'Date' may not be desirable - it's possibly just a Figma label. This is a design decision that should be confirmed.
issues.fix: Confirm with the designer whether 'Date (January 19, 2026)' is the desired format, or if it should just be 'January 19, 2026' without the 'Date' prefix.

issues.description: Testimonial card uses wp_kses_post() for message output instead of esc_html(). At card-testimonial.php line 17, the message meta field is output with wp_kses_post() which allows HTML tags. If testimonial messages should be plain text (as typical for user-submitted testimonials), esc_html() would be more secure. However, if the message is expected to contain formatting (bold, italic, paragraphs), wp_kses_post() is correct.
issues.fix: Use esc_html() if testimonial messages should be plain text, or keep wp_kses_post() if rich text formatting is expected. Document the decision.

issues.description: Four prettier formatting errors in blocks/Archive/index.tsx (lines 21, 31, 40, 58). These are CRLF line ending issues and long-line formatting issues in the variation description strings. Auto-fixable with 'bun run lint:fix'.
issues.fix: Run 'bun run lint:fix' to auto-fix the prettier formatting issues.

queryVarAnalysis.mappingCorrectness: The taxonomy-to-query-var mapping in $post_type_config uses the taxonomy slug (e.g., 'category' => 'topic', 'department' => 'department'). The PHP iterates $enabled_taxonomies and uses the slug value for get_query_var(), which matches the registered query vars.
